models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.description: Gemini 3.1 Pro is Google's most advanced Gemini model, with native multimodal capabilities for image generation, understanding, and editing. Supports 1M+ token context window. Image generation quality is greatly improved, with outstanding visual reasoning tasks.
strengths: Image generation reaches Google's best level

models.name: Seedance series
models.description: AI video generation platform by ByteDance. Seedance focuses on video generation (text-to-video, image-to-video), while the Seedream series focuses on image generation. Seedance 2.0 shows significant improvements in semantic understanding and motion coherence. Seedream 4.x/5.x performs excellently in image generation benchmarks (e.g., GenEval, DPG-Bench, HPSv3), with Seedream 4.0 reaching cutting-edge levels in text rendering and complex scene handling.
strengths: Seedream high image quality, precise text rendering
limitations: Model weights not open-sourced, only available via API
Seedream series has limitations in specific artistic styles
Domestic users must comply with content review policies
notSuitableFor: Local deployment requirements (all are cloud API products)
Professional film production requiring full control over the generation process

models.name: gpt-image-2
models.description: OpenAI's state-of-the-art image generation model, integrated into the Responses API. Understands text and images, uses broad world knowledge to generate images with strong instruction following and contextual awareness. Can generate images of real-world objects with lifelike details without needing reference images.
strengths: Excellent instruction following and prompt adherence
Strong world knowledge for realistic depictions
Can use both text and image inputs for generation/editing
Integrated natively with the Responses API alongside gpt-5.5
limitations: May have limitations with very complex scenes or fine text rendering
Safety filters may block some creative content
suitableFor: General-purpose image generation from text prompts
Visual creative work requiring world knowledge

models.name: Kling video generation series
models.description: Kling is an AI video generation model by Kwai, supporting text-to-video and image-to-video. The underlying architecture is fully upgraded; Video 3.0 natively supports deep multimodal instruction parsing and cross-task fusion. Supports ultra-long video precise storyboarding and feature decoupling for audio-video synchronization, achieving dual binding of visual subjects and auditory timbres. The main advantages of Kling 1.6/2.0 era are realistic video generation and physical motion consistency.
strengths: Video physics motion logic is reasonable, high consistency of characters/objects
Ultra-long video generation and precise storyboarding capabilities
Supports multiple video ratios and resolutions
limitations: Deformation may occur in complex action scenes
Pure commercial product, model weights not open-sourced
May not meet expectations when processing unconventional prompts
Better understanding of Chinese prompts than English
notSuitableFor: Precise control of fine animation in each frame
Ultra-realistic close-ups of human body (occasional issues with hands and other details)

Can generate images of real-world objects with lifelike details without needing reference images.
strengths: Excellent instruction following and prompt adherence
Strong world knowledge for realistic depictions
Can use both text and image inputs for generation/editing
Integrated natively with the Responses API alongside gpt-5.5
limitations: May have limitations with very complex scenes or fine text rendering
Safety filters may block some creative content
suitableFor: General-purpose image generation from text prompts
Visual creative work requiring world knowledge

models.name: Seedance series
models.description: AI video generation platform by ByteDance. Seedance focuses on video generation (text-to-video, image-to-video), while the Seedream series focuses on image generation. Seedance 2.0 shows significant improvements in semantic understanding and motion coherence. Seedream 4.x/5.x performs excellently in image generation benchmarks (e.g., GenEval, DPG-Bench, HPSv3), with Seedream 4.0 reaching cutting-edge levels in text rendering and complex scene handling.
strengths: Seedream high image quality, precise text rendering
limitations: Model weights not open-sourced, only available via API
Seedream series has limitations in specific artistic styles
Domestic users must comply with content review policies
notSuitableFor: Local deployment requirements (all are cloud API products)
Professional film production requiring full control over the generation process

models.name: Kling video generation series
models.description: Kling is an AI video generation model by Kwai, supporting text-to-video and image-to-video. The underlying architecture is fully upgraded; Video 3.0 natively supports deep multimodal instruction parsing and cross-task fusion. Supports ultra-long video precise storyboarding and feature decoupling for audio-video synchronization, achieving dual binding of visual subjects and auditory timbres. The main advantages of Kling 1.6/2.0 era are realistic video generation and physical motion consistency.
strengths: Video physics motion logic is reasonable, high consistency of characters/objects
Ultra-long video generation and precise storyboarding capabilities
Supports multiple video ratios and resolutions
limitations: Deformation may occur in complex action scenes
Pure commercial product, model weights not open-sourced
May not meet expectations when processing unconventional prompts
Better understanding of Chinese prompts than English
notSuitableFor: Precise control of fine animation in each frame
Ultra-realistic close-ups of human body (occasional issues with hands and other details)

models.name: Google Gemini Image
models.description: Google Gemini Image is the image generation capability built into the Gemini model. Based on Imagen technology, it generates images directly through natural language. Seamlessly integrated with Gemini text, it supports chat-based interaction, editing, and retouching.

models.name: Nano Banana Pro

models.name: Stable Diffusion 1.5 (SD1.5)
models.description: The most classic Stable Diffusion model. 0.86B UNet + 0.12B CLIP text encoder. Despite having the fewest parameters, thanks to the vast community ecosystem, SD1.5 has the most LoRA, ControlNet, T2I-Adapter, and other resources. Supports 512×512 base resolution. It is currently the model with the widest plugin and tool compatibility.

Minimum inference requirements (4GB VRAM is sufficient)
Small model size, fast download and deployment
Has accumulated a vast number of fine-tuned models and checkpoints
limitations: Image quality far behind current models
Only 512×512 native resolution (quality degrades after upscaling)
Poor performance on complex compositions and multi-person scenes
suitableFor: Scenarios requiring compatibility with a large number of old LoRA/ControlNet
Reuse of mature fine-tuned models in specific domains
notSuitableFor: New applications requiring precise prompt following

models.name: HiDream-O1-Image
models.description: HiDream's latest generation image generation model, based on the Pixel-level Unified Transformer (UiT) architecture. No external VAE or text encoder required; all modalities unified in a shared token space. Supports text-to-image, instruction editing, subject-driven personalization, and storyboard generation. With 8B parameters, it achieves or surpasses larger models (FLUX.2 Dev 24B+32B, Qwen-Image 7B+20B) on GenEval (0.90), DPG-Bench (89.83), and HPSv3 (10.37). Built-in Reasoning-Driven Prompt Agent (based on Gemma-4-31B).
strengths: Unified architecture, no external VAE/text encoder required
Top-tier performance on four benchmarks: GenEval/DPG-Bench/HPSv3/CVTG-2K
Supports long text rendering (multi-region, multi-language text)
Supports layout and skeleton condition control
limitations: 8B model inference still requires large VRAM
Ecosystem is still in early development
Inference speed slower than distilled models (Full requires 50 steps)
suitableFor: Text rendering (posters, titles, complex text layouts)

models.name: Recraft
models.description: A professional designer-oriented AI image generation and editing platform. Headquartered in London, founded in 2022. The core model claims to 'think in design language,' excelling in generating design elements such as vectors, icons, logos, 3D graphics, illustrations, and high-precision text rendering. Supports text-to-image generation, inpainting, background removal, image vectorization, and upsampling. Available through the Recraft Studio platform and API, also integrated via Figma/Framer/Chrome plugins. Over 3 million users, generating over 350 million images. Additionally integrates 20+ other models (Flux, Nano Banana, Ideogram, GPT Image, HiDream, Seedream, etc.).
strengths: Excellent image text rendering capabilities (any length and size)
Specialized functions for graphic design scenarios (precise position control)
Supports CMYK printing colors and DPI control (professional design output)
Rich editing tools (background removal, vector conversion, upscaling, etc.)
Plugin integration with Figma/Google Docs, etc.
V4 Pro version image quality excels in multiple categories
limitations: Purely closed-source product (API/Studio), cannot be deployed locally
Vector AI generation is not true vector paths (it is bitmap to SVG)
Versions prior to V4.1 perform average on certain artistic styles
Understanding of specific cultural contexts is sometimes inaccurate
suitableFor: Daily design work for graphic designers
Social media images and ad banners
Poster design requiring precise text control
E-commerce product image design and editing
notSuitableFor: Requires secondary development on a fully open-source base

models.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5 (SD3.5)
models.description: Stability AI's latest generation stable diffusion model (successor to SD3). Adopts MMDiT (Multi-Modal Diffusion Transformer) architecture, comprehensively surpassing SDXL on GenEval/DPG-Bench. SD3.5 Large has 8.1B MMDiT + 5.5B T5-XXL encoder. Supports 1024×1024 output, with text rendering capability far stronger than SDXL.
strengths: Significantly improved prompt adherence compared to SDXL
Image quality and detail superior to SD3 Medium
Open source and commercially available (Stability AI Community License)
limitations: Community resources and LoRA lag behind SDXL/FLUX
Primarily API/cloud-based, local deployment is relatively complex
Image quality in some scenarios still falls short of FLUX/HiDream
notSuitableFor: Requires maximum community resources and tool support
Commercial needs under MIT open-source license

models.name: Stable Diffusion XL (SDXL)
models.description: Stable Diffusion model launched by Stability AI, 0.81B UNet + 2.6B parameters. Compared to SD1.5/2.1, it significantly improves image quality, supports 1024×1024 native resolution, and has better composition ability and style diversity. The base model has spawned various accelerated variants such as SDXL Turbo (one-step generation) and SDXL-Lightning (4-step generation).
strengths: Extremely rich ecosystem (LoRA, ControlNet, plugins, etc.)
Vast community pre-trained models and resources
limitations: Prompt following ability weaker than SD3.5/FLUX/new models
Performance is average for complex compositions and multi-person scenes
Already belongs to previous-generation technology (relative to SD3.5/FLUX)
suitableFor: Broad artistic creation and creative design
Compatible use of extensive community resources
notSuitableFor: Pursuing high-quality output with the latest technology

models.name: ACE-Step 1.5
models.description: The strongest open-source AI music generation model, supporting local operation. Has 10,850+ stars on GitHub. Supports 4+ minute full-length song generation (including vocals and lyrics), instrumental mode, style tags (BPM/key/time signature), reference audio, audio cover, paragraph repainting, and other features. Called the 'open-source Suno/Killer' by the community. Supports various hardware devices such as Mac (MPS), AMD, Intel, CUDA. Open source under MIT license.
strengths: Local operation, data privacy and security
Music quality comparable to commercial services like Suno/Udio
Supports precise control of BPM/key/time signature
Active community and rich UI frontends
limitations: Slow inference speed (generating a song takes several minutes)
Vocal quality still lags behind professional recordings
Limited accuracy in complex orchestration and multi-instrument separation
The pronunciation and rhythm of Chinese lyrics are sometimes not natural enough
suitableFor: Learning and researching AI music generation

models.name: ElevenLabs
models.description: World-leading AI voice and audio platform. Core capabilities include: ultra-realistic text-to-speech (TTS, 70+ languages), voice cloning (Instant Voice Cloning and Professional Voice Cloning), speech-to-text (STT), AI dubbing, AI music generation, and ElevenAgents (voice dialogue agents). Widely used in audiobooks, podcasts, advertisements, game character dubbing, customer service, etc. The Flash model achieves near real-time TTS latency.
strengths: TTS voice quality and naturalness are industry-leading
Multiple voice styles (narrative, advertising, character, etc.)
High-precision voice cloning (Instant PVC requires only seconds of audio)
Supports emotion control and voice parameter adjustment
limitations: Quality in some languages is lower than English
Voice cloning has ethical controversies and abuse risks
suitableFor: Voice for advertising and marketing content
notSuitableFor: Scenarios with extremely high concurrency and limited budget

models.name: Stable Diffusion XL (SDXL)
models.description: Stable Diffusion model launched by Stability AI, 0.81B UNet + 2.6B parameters. Compared to SD1.5/2.1, it significantly improves image quality, supports 1024×1024 native resolution, and has better composition ability and style diversity. The base model has spawned various accelerated variants such as SDXL Turbo (one-step generation) and SDXL-Lightning (4-step generation).
strengths: Extremely rich ecosystem (LoRA, ControlNet, plugins, etc.)
Vast community pre-trained models and resources
limitations: Prompt following ability weaker than SD3.5/FLUX/new models
Performance is average for complex compositions and multi-person scenes
Already belongs to previous-generation technology (relative to SD3.5/FLUX)
suitableFor: Broad artistic creation and creative design
Compatible use of extensive community resources
notSuitableFor: Pursuing high-quality output with the latest technology

models.name: Stable Audio
models.description: Stability AI's AI audio generation model series. Stable Audio 2.0/3.0 supports text-to-audio generation of complete music tracks, sound effects, and ambient sounds. Stable Audio Open 1.0 is the open-source version (restricted access). Supports up to 4-5 minutes of music generation, covering multiple genres and styles. Professional-quality audio can be generated through natural language descriptions.
strengths: High-quality AI audio generation (commercial-grade music and sound effects)
Supports text description to control style and mood
Stable Audio 3 supports higher quality and longer output
Open-source version available for local deployment (Open series)
Sound effects (SFX) dedicated model with high precision
limitations: Open source version requires access permission
Generated music still has shortcomings in complex structures
Limited by training data, some styles may perform average
Voice generation quality is inferior to professional TTS models
suitableFor: BGM for film and video projects
Audio content and podcast audio elements
Sound effect design and ambient sound generation
notSuitableFor: Scenarios requiring precise customization of human voice/singing
Professional studio production with extremely high audio quality requirements

models.name: Hunyuan3D
models.description: Tencent's open-source 3D asset generation model series. Generates high-resolution 3D models from text prompts or single images. Features include PBR material generation, multi-view texture generation, and Blender addon support. State-of-the-art in open-source 3D generation.
limitations: Requires significant GPU memory for full resolution
Generation quality variable on complex organic shapes
Texture quality may need enhancement pass

models.name: Tripo
models.description: Tripo is an AI 3D generation platform created by VAST AI Research. Open-source research models: TripoSG (1.5B parameter Rectified Flow Transformer), TripoSR (single-image fast 3D), TripoSF (SparseFlex high resolution). Provides Python SDK/Blender plugin/ComfyUI node/MCP server. TripoSG has published papers at top conferences such as CVPR.
strengths: Supports multiple input types: photo, cartoon, sketch
limitations: High-quality models require high GPU memory

models.name: Tripo P1
models.description: Tripo P1 is a new generation generation model from the Tripo 3D platform, positioned for performance priority, focusing more on actual deployment and speed optimization. Inherits the image understanding and 3D reconstruction capabilities of the Tripo series.
strengths: Fast inference speed, suitable for real-time applications

models.name: Tripo P1

strengths: Fast inference speed, suitable for real-time applications

models.name: Rodin
models.description: Rodin is an AI 3D generation model created by former Google researchers, known for text-to-3D and image-to-3D conversion. Acquired by Genies (digital avatar platform) in 2023. The model focuses on generating high-fidelity 3D models, especially character and avatar-related assets, from a single image or text prompt.
strengths: High quality for generating 3D characters/avatars from a single image
Deep integration with the Genies ecosystem

models.name: TripoSplat
models.description: TripoSplat is a 3D generation model by VAST AI Research based on 3D Gaussian Splatting. Using 3D Gaussian radiance field representation for content creation enables higher-quality view synthesis and detail presentation.
strengths: Strong detail expressiveness in Gaussian Splatting
limitations: Technology is relatively new, maturity to be verified
notSuitableFor: Requires traditional workflow with mesh models

models.name: Meshy
models.description: Meshy is one of the most popular AI 3D model generation platforms in the industry, supporting text-to-3D and image-to-3D generation. It provides REST API and plugins for mainstream 3D platforms such as Blender, Unity, Unreal Engine, 3ds Max, Maya, and Godot. It can generate production-ready 3D assets in seconds, with built-in post-processing functions, supporting industrial standard formats such as FBX/GLB/OBJ/STL.
strengths: Fast text/image to 3D generation, in seconds
limitations: Requires internet connection, not a local open-source model
Complex topology and high-precision details still have room for improvement
Advanced features and API require payment
